Refreshing output…

WMS-Rot Suite

WMS-Prep

Prepare geometries and export WMS-Rot inputs with Gaussian TauP/Phi reconstruction, selectable final representation, and optional quadrupole data.

Preparing GAUSSIAN gjf

This section detects the principal-axis representation of the input geometry. Representation changes are disabled, so XYZ and GAUSSIAN exports keep the detected input representation.

In the tables below, each representation shows which rotational axis is written on the exported X, Y, and Z coordinates.

Right-handed set
Rep X Y Z
Irbca
IIrcab
IIIrabc
Left-handed set
Rep X Y Z
Ilcba
IIlacb
IIIlbac

Internal Z/X/Y assignment used by the tool: Ir = a/b/c, IIr = b/c/a, IIIr = c/a/b, Il = a/c/b, IIl = b/a/c, IIIl = c/b/a.

Load XYZ File

Paste XYZ

SMILES

RDKit adds explicit hydrogens and generates an initial coordinate set.

Detected Representation

Undetermined

Analysis

Representation changes are disabled on this page. XYZ and GJF exports keep the detected input representation. 

No file loaded.

Output XYZ

Downloads


Generate WMS Input from GAUSSIAN log

Load Gaussian logs and XYZ files to populate Geometry, Quartic, Sextic, and DVib sources in the flow board and generate the final WMS-Rot input.

  • Gaussian logs rebuild quartic constants from TauP and sextic constants from inertial Phi abc tensors.
  • The final export can be generated in Ir (quasi-prolate) or IIIl (quasi-oblate).
  • Both Watson reductions, A and S, are written to the final WMS-Rot input.
  • If a Gaussian log contains nuclear quadrupole tensors, they are extracted and added to the generated WMS-Rot input.
  • XYZ sources contribute geometry only and use default dipoles 1.0 / 1.0 / 1.0.

• If one loaded source already contains a complete pipeline, the tool auto-connects that set first.

Quartic, sextic, and quadrupole data are re-expressed in the selected final representation. Rotational constants, dipole, and DVib are always exported in a fixed a/b/c ordering.

Loaded sources
  • No source loaded.
